title: verbascum nitidum var. thyrsoideum
intro: Verbascum nitidum var. thyrsoideum, also known as the silky mullein, is a plant species in the mullein family native to Europe, Western Asia, and North Africa. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma, due to its anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties. The plant is also valued for its health benefits, including reducing inflammation, soothing coughs, and promoting relaxation. The silky mullein can be prepared into herbal teas, tinctures, and infusions, or used topically as a poultice to take advantage of its medicinal properties.
